reforms.description: The Swedish government has introduced a special depreciation allowance of 20 percent for investment in machinery and equipment, effective from January 1, 1972, to stimulate business investment during an economic downturn.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The depreciation allowance incentivizes businesses to invest, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into effect as of January 1, 1972.
reforms.importance_rationale: While it is a significant reform aimed at stimulating investment, it does not represent a structural change in the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The special depreciation allowance in 1972 may also help to sustain the level of investment demand in face of the present downturn.

reforms.description: The Swedish government is providing high grants for environmental investments, which are set at 75 percent, to incentivize businesses to invest in environmentally friendly technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The high grants for environmental investments encourage businesses to adopt sustainable technologies, which can enhance innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The status of implementation is not specified in the provided text.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly supports environmental innovation but does not fundamentally alter the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The present high grants for environmental investments (75 per cent) should also provide an extra incentive.
